title: Israel halts Russian grain ship amid stolen Ukrainian wheat row
content: After days of diplomatic row between Ukraine and Israel, Israeli grain importer Zenziper said it is postponing the unloading of the grain on board the ship said to be carrying stolen Ukrainian grain on behalf of Russia.
snippet: After days of diplomatic row between Ukraine and Israel, Israeli grain importer Zenziper said it is postponing the unloading of the grain on board the ship said to be carrying stolen Ukrainian grain o

title: Global press freedom at lowest level in 25 years, Reporters Without Borders report shows
content: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.
snippet: In its latest report, RSF has warned that press freedom is at a 25-year low, with over half the world in “difficult” or “very serious” status and few “good” countries.

title: The "giant" heads home: After war missions and Maduro capture, a record return
content: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is set to return to the US after completing missions that included operations in the Iran war and the US mission in Venezuela that saw the capture of Nicolas Maduro.
snippet: The world’s largest aircraft carrier is set to return to the US after completing missions that included operations in the Iran war and the US mission in Venezuela that saw the capture of Nicolas Madur

title: PSG vs Bayern - a semifinal for the ages and football at its very best
content: In this special football edition of The Ring, we deconstruct a Champions League game that will go down in history as one of the best ever. Nine goals - can top quality football really be any better? Our experts also weigh in on what else is moving Europe's most popular sport.
snippet: In this special football edition of The Ring, we deconstruct a Champions League game that will go down in history as one of the best ever. Nine goals - can top quality football really be any better? O

title: Switzerland or Italy? Who covers medical costs after the Crans-Montana fire?
content: After the deadly New Year’s Eve fire in the Swiss resort of Crans-Montana, tensions are rising after Switzerland sent invoices to the victims' families. What’s behind the bills?
snippet: After the deadly New Year’s Eve fire in the Swiss resort of Crans-Montana, tensions are rising after Switzerland sent invoices to the victims' families. What’s behind the bills?

title: Spain's PM 'was at level one' in scheme to rig public contracts, defendant in graft case says
content: The Socialists' turmoil has rocked relations with their far-left junior coalition partner Sumar and an array of fringe and regional separatist parties whose support is essential to pass legislation.
snippet: The Socialists' turmoil has rocked relations with their far-left junior coalition partner Sumar and an array of fringe and regional separatist parties whose support is essential to pass legislation.

title: China pushes EU capitals to scrap 'Made in Europe' law or face retaliation
content: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.
snippet: As EU countries debate the European Commission’s proposal, Beijing is urging national governments to abandon the planned law. Otherwise, China says it will take countermeasures.

title: Italy says it has filed to be civil party in probe into Swiss bar fire that killed 41
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.
snippet: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has vowed justice for Italian victims while Italy has opened its own investigation into the fire even as the Swiss probe continues.

title: Magyar says EU money to arrive in Hungary 'soon' after meeting with von der Leyen
content: Hungary’s incoming prime minister hailed "productive" meetings in Brussels as he races to unlock billions in EU funding ahead of a summer deadline. Commission President von der Leyen vows to keep working together.
snippet: Hungary’s incoming prime minister hailed "productive" meetings in Brussels as he races to unlock billions in EU funding ahead of a summer deadline. Commission President von der Leyen vows to keep work

title: Europol GRIMM taskforce nets 280 arrests as 'violence for hire' spreads across Europe
content: The taskforce sees Europol collaborate with police forces in 11 countries and has already identified more than 1,400 people linked to violence as a service.
snippet: The taskforce sees Europol collaborate with police forces in 11 countries and has already identified more than 1,400 people linked to violence as a service.

title: Ukraine says it will open arms exports with 'Drone Deals,' but not to all countries
content: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise.
snippet: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday that Ukraine is set to open its arms exports via so-called drone deals, but not all countries will have access to Kyiv's battlefield tested expertise

title: Hungary's incoming PM Péter Magyar offers to meet Ukraine's president in June
content: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.
snippet: Relations between the two neighbours hit rock bottom before Hungary's 12 April election that saw Magyar beat long-time nationalist premier Viktor Orbán.

title: European Parliament demands €200 billion extra for EU budget in hardball talks
content: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify for 2028-2034 cycle.
snippet: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify

title: German bid to rescue 'Timmy' the whale stranded off Baltic coast passes key hurdle
content: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.
snippet: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.

title: Ukraine says it shot down 33,000 Russian drones in March, a new monthly record
content: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.
snippet: Ukraine has developed cutting-edge and battle-tested drone technology that has proved essential in holding back Russia’s bigger army and has drawn military interest from around the world.

title: Ukraine summons Israel's ambassador over allegedly stolen grain being shipped to Haifa
content: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.
snippet: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.

title: Fact-checking claims that Spain's legalised migrants can move to other EU countries
content: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.
snippet: Under Sánchez's programme, undocumented migrants who have lived in Spain for at least five months can apply for a one-year temporary residence permit, which has opened the door to disinformation.

title: UN human rights office rebukes Sweden for deporting disabled child twice to Albania
content: The committee, a body of 18 independent experts, monitors implementation of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights by its state parties and conducts regular country reviews.
snippet: The committee, a body of 18 independent experts, monitors implementation of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights by its state parties and conducts regular country reviews.

title: Italy extradites alleged Chinese hacker to US accused of spying for Beijing during COVID-19 pandemic
content: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.
snippet: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.

title: Belgium seeks to take control of nuclear reactors in move to control energy supplies
content: Brussels believes the solution to soaring energy prices as a result of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the war in the Middle East is to permanently transition away from fossil fuels.
snippet: Brussels believes the solution to soaring energy prices as a result of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the war in the Middle East is to permanently transition away from fossil fuels.

title: Royal recruits boost volunteers as the Netherlands builds up its military reservists
content: The royal seal of approval, together with recruitment campaigns running across the media, has proven so successful that the military is now working overtime to arm, train and accommodate the influx of newcomers.

title: Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire
content: After the Easter truce, Moscow is now proposing a Victory Day parade ceasefire as the Kremlin is concerned over Ukraine’s long-range striking capabilities. Moscow has already scaled down the parade, where for the first time in years, Russian armoured columns will not be rolling on the Red Square.

title: Israel intercepts Gaza-bound flotilla, arresting 175 activists
content: Isreal has intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters near Greece, arresting 175 activists, with officials saying blockade enforcement is vital to stop weapons reaching the territory.
snippet: Isreal has intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters near Greece, arresting 175 activists, with officials saying blockade enforcement is vital to stop weapons reaching the territor

title: EU failed to 'listen' in Sahel, envoy admits as influence wanes
content: EU envoy admits Brussels misread Sahel dynamics, warning that military-first strategies and one-way diplomacy have eroded influence as Russia and China deepen their foothold in the region.
snippet: EU envoy admits Brussels misread Sahel dynamics, warning that military-first strategies and one-way diplomacy have eroded influence as Russia and China deepen their foothold in the region.

title: 'We cannot give Russia legitimacy through sports events', says MEP Ville Niinistö
content: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".
snippet: Ville Niinistö, MEP for the Greens, believes that Russia is now "weaker than a year ago" and says the country is using its foreign connections to "legitimise their war crimes in Ukraine".

title: From statements to actions: Here's which European countries are spending the most on defence
content: Military spending by European NATO members last year rose faster than at any time since 1953.
snippet: Military spending by European NATO members last year rose faster than at any time since 1953.

title: Italian woman faces jail in Egypt for adultery as custody battle intensifies
content: Sentenced to six months in prison for adultery in Egypt, Nessy Guerra is caught up in a complex case of alleged abuse, a daughter stranded in the country and the limits of Italian intervention.
snippet: Sentenced to six months in prison for adultery in Egypt, Nessy Guerra is caught up in a complex case of alleged abuse, a daughter stranded in the country and the limits of Italian intervention.

title: Israeli forces intercept Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters near Greece
content: The Global Sumud Flotilla activists said on Thursday Israeli soldiers boarded the ships, damaged engines and communications devices in an effort to force them to reverse course.
snippet: The Global Sumud Flotilla activists said on Thursday Israeli soldiers boarded the ships, damaged engines and communications devices in an effort to force them to reverse course.

title: Europe Today: Péter Magyar first visit to Brussels and China-EU tensions rising

title: Czechia: A small country with big nuclear energy ambitions
content: Czechia stands out in Europe for its bold pursuit of nuclear energy sovereignty and its ambition to lead in atomic power as it cuts reliance on fossil fuels.
snippet: Czechia stands out in Europe for its bold pursuit of nuclear energy sovereignty and its ambition to lead in atomic power as it cuts reliance on fossil fuels.

title: Sweden confiscates false-flagged Russian 'shadow fleet' ship, prosecutors say
content: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union.
snippet: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union.

title: MEPs with new-borns can delegate their vote – but only if they are women
content: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.
snippet: The European Parliament has approved a rule allowing proxy voting for new mothers before and after childbirth. However, it raises questions about gender equality, as it does not cover male MEPs.

title: Second US ambassador to Ukraine steps down in less than one year
content: The acting US ambassador to Ukraine is to step down from her post in Kyiv and retire, the US State Department announced. Reports have indicated her reasons for leaving the job are related to the Trump administration's approach to Ukraine.

title: EU loosens state aid rules as Middle East crisis hits energy costs
content: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.
snippet: Brussels loosens state aid rules as Middle East turmoil drives up fuel and fertiliser costs, allowing governments to cushion farmers, hauliers and industry with emergency subsidies.

title: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia
content: A Ukrainian source told Euronews that "if Israel responded to other requests via diplomatic channels, there would have been no need for tweets," after Israel's foreign minister lashed out over what he called "Twitter diplomacy."

title: HAYI group claims responsibility for stabbing two Jewish men in north London
content: The incident comes in the wake of a spate of arson attacks on synagogues and Jewish community sites in north London in recent weeks.
snippet: The incident comes in the wake of a spate of arson attacks on synagogues and Jewish community sites in north London in recent weeks.

title: Man accused of spying for Russia arrested in Berlin, prosecutors say
content: The suspect allegedly gave his Russian intelligence handler information about German military aid for Ukraine and photos of NATO military convoys moving along motorways.
snippet: The suspect allegedly gave his Russian intelligence handler information about German military aid for Ukraine and photos of NATO military convoys moving along motorways.

title: Kubilius pushes Europen Defence Union but critics brand it a distraction
content: EU defence chief Andrius Kubilius is pushing a European Defence Union to tie Ukraine with its European allies, but the idea is already under fire, with one MEP warning that it risks distracting from the current job of bulletproofing the EU.

title: 'The EU is not the little sister of the US,' European Parliament Vice-President Wilmès says
content: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.
snippet: The EU and the US should talk to each other as equal partners, European Parliament Vice-President and former Belgian Prime Minister Sophie Wilmès told Euronews' morning show Europe Today.

title: Conspiracy theories abound after White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
content: US President Donald Trump is no stranger to theories alleging that attempts on his life are false flag operations.
snippet: US President Donald Trump is no stranger to theories alleging that attempts on his life are false flag operations.

title: EU backs ending new oil and gas drilling
content: The war in the Middle East may have driven up oil and gas demand, but the world should still ditch new fossil fuel drilling, EU climate chief Wopke Hoekstra has said, insisting on "affordable, reliable, homegrown clean energy."

title: How engaged are young Europeans in online civic and political life?
content: Online platforms are becoming the new political arena for Europe's youth. With their online engagement higher than in older groups, how do countries compare across the continent?
snippet: Online platforms are becoming the new political arena for Europe's youth. With their online engagement higher than in older groups, how do countries compare across the continent?

title: EU Commissioner Mînzatu counting on Romanian 'pro-European stance' despite feared eurosceptic turn

title: Kyiv vows response as Russia ships stolen grain to multiple countries

snippet: As the diplomatic row between Ukraine and Israel continues to escalate, Kyiv said there are also other countries buying its stolen grain from Russia-occupied territories.

title: King Charles III lauds ‘indispensable’ US-UK alliance at White House state dinner
content: During the White House state dinner on Tuesday evening, King Charles III highlighted “one of the most consequential alliances in human history” in a largely jovial and lighthearted speech.
snippet: During the White House state dinner on Tuesday evening, King Charles III highlighted “one of the most consequential alliances in human history” in a largely jovial and lighthearted speech.

title: Newsletter: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar in Brussels to unlock EU funds
content: The incoming Hungarian leader is pressed for time as he seeks to unlock billions of euros in EU cash before an August deadline.
snippet: The incoming Hungarian leader is pressed for time as he seeks to unlock billions of euros in EU cash before an August deadline.

title: Europe revives renewables and nuclear to address the energy crisis
content: Brussels is looking for a long-term fix, putting both renewables and nuclear back on the table after the Strait of Hormuz closure sent energy bills soaring. Watch the video!

title: Magyar to meet von der Leyen as Hungary races to unlock EU cash
content: Incoming Hungarian Prime Minister lands in Brussels for what is described as "informal, but urgent talks" with Commission chief von der Leyen. Budapest is facing a tight deadline to unlock EU funding choked during the Orbán era.

title: China hawks are gaining ground in the Commission. Will EU countries follow?
content: The European Commission's services are sizing up tools to counter Chinese rivals, including the Anti-Coercion Instrument. But despite mounting pressure from Beijing, the issue is still in its early stages — and EU member states remain divided.

title: Brussels instructs EU countries to be tough on net-zero shipping goals at key IMO talks
content: The high-level talks come after a disastrous meeting in October, when a proposal to tax shipping emissions was blocked by the United States. Within the EU, Greece, Italy and Malta remain hesitant to support the proposal.

title: Santorini announces new restrictions after scientists flagged volcanic activity
content: New restrictions, including the closure of a hiking trail, could remain in place until 2027 as scientists are concerned over volcanic and seismic hazards.
snippet: New restrictions, including the closure of a hiking trail, could remain in place until 2027 as scientists are concerned over volcanic and seismic hazards.

title: Louis Arnaud, former French hostage in Iran, describes Evin prison as 'the den of evil'
content: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.
snippet: Speaking to Euronews, Louis Arnaud says the memories of Evin Prison continue to haunt him, as he recalls a place where captives are crammed into windowless cells and where the lights never go out.

title: Ukraine-Israel row deepens over Russian shipments of stolen grain
content: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories in southern Ukraine.
snippet: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories
